title: Elatinoides aegyptiaca
taxonomy: Elatinoides aegyptiaca is classified within the family Elatinaceae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Elatinaceae is part of the larger clade of core eudicots. Elatinoides aegyptiaca's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants. This classification is based on molecular and morphological characteristics.

intro: Elatinoides aegyptiaca, also known as Egyptian wormseed, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, commonly found in Egypt and parts of Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can help to treat various ailments such as skin infections, wounds, and parasitic infestations. The bioactive constituents of Elatinoides aegyptiaca include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections.
